definitions.definition: Dissolution of a partnership firm occurs when the partnership is brought to an end either by an agreement between the partners or by operation of law. This can be either voluntary or involuntary.
definitions.example: For example, when two partners, Rohan and Aryan, decide to end their business partnership due to differences, it is a voluntary dissolution.

definitions.definition: Voluntary dissolution occurs when all the partners agree to dissolve the partnership firm. This can be done by a partnership deed or a resolution passed at a meeting of the partners.
definitions.example: When a partner retires or decides to exit the business, the remaining partners may agree to dissolve the firm voluntarily.

definitions.definition: Involuntary dissolution occurs when the partnership firm is dissolved due to circumstances beyond the control of the partners, such as death of a partner, insolvency, or a court order.
definitions.example: If a partner goes bankrupt, the firm may be dissolved involuntarily due to the inability to continue business.

definitions.definition: Dissolution by mutual consent occurs when all the partners agree to dissolve the partnership firm. This is a type of voluntary dissolution.
definitions.example: When all the partners agree to sell their business and end the partnership, it is a dissolution by mutual consent.

definitions.definition: Dissolution by operation of law occurs when the partnership firm is dissolved due to circumstances beyond the control of the partners, such as death of a partner or insolvency.
definitions.example: If a partner dies, the firm may be dissolved by operation of law, as the business can no longer be continued.

definitions.definition: A liquidator is an individual or firm appointed to wind up the affairs of the dissolved partnership firm and distribute the assets among the partners.
definitions.example: The court may appoint a liquidator to manage the dissolution process and distribute the assets fairly.

definitions.definition: A statement of affairs is a document prepared by the liquidator to show the assets, liabilities, and financial position of the dissolved partnership firm.
definitions.example: The liquidator prepares a statement of affairs to provide a detailed picture of the firm's financial situation at the time of dissolution.

definitions.definition: A final realisation account is a type of account prepared to show the final distribution of assets among the partners after the dissolution of the partnership firm.
definitions.example: The final realisation account helps to determine the share of each partner in the firm's assets and liabilities.

definitions.definition: Winding-up is the process of liquidating the assets of the dissolved partnership firm and distributing them among the partners.
definitions.example: The winding-up process involves the sale of assets, payment of debts, and distribution of the remaining amount to the partners.

definitions.definition: A dissolution account is a type of account prepared to show the final distribution of assets among the partners after the dissolution of the partnership firm.
definitions.example: The dissolution account helps to determine the share of each partner in the firm's assets and liabilities.

definitions.definition: Loss account is prepared when the assets of the firm are less than the liabilities.
definitions.example: If the firm's assets are less than its liabilities, the loss account is prepared to calculate the amount of loss.

definitions.definition: Surplus account is prepared when the assets of the firm are more than the liabilities.
definitions.example: If the firm's assets are more than its liabilities, the surplus account is prepared to calculate the amount of surplus.

definitions.definition: Revaluation account is prepared when the assets or liabilities of the firm are revalued at the time of dissolution.
definitions.example: If the assets of the firm are revalued at the time of dissolution, the revaluation account is prepared to show the difference in value.

definitions.definition: Realisation account is prepared to show the realisation of assets of the firm at the time of dissolution.
definitions.example: The realisation account shows the sale of assets and the amount realised from them.

definitions.definition: Partner's capital account is prepared to show the initial capital and the final capital of each partner at the time of dissolution.
definitions.example: The partner's capital account shows the amount of capital contributed and withdrawn by each partner.

definitions.definition: Partner's current account is prepared to show the profits and losses of the firm and the drawings made by each partner at the time of dissolution.
definitions.example: The partner's current account shows the share of profits and losses of each partner.
